summary: Draft PR for `src/melee/pl/plbonus.c` that replaced the `/// #fn_80039618` asm placeholder with a large C reconstruction of player bonus/stat flag logic, refined `pl_804D6470_t` field types in `src/melee/pl/types.h`, protected `fn_80038700` from inlining, and adjusted decimal digit extraction in `fn_8003CC84`. The PR was closed unmerged, but the decomp.dev bot reported meaningful matching progress: `fn_80039618` improved from 0.00% to 99.67%, `fn_8003CC84` reached 100%, and plbonus `.data`/`.sdata2` reached 100%. No human review feedback or PR body was available in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented `fn_80039618(int player)` in C, replacing a placeholder with roughly 300 lines of bonus condition logic based on `pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t`, `plActionStats`, `HSD_GObj`, stale move table fields, attack/hit counters, item-thrown counters, KO data, fall data, and global thresholds from `pl_804D6470`.

Added multiple `setFlag(player, ...)` paths for bonus IDs driven by totals and ratios such as aerial-only hits, special-only hits, low-move-only hits, thrown item hit ratios, no attacks, no falls, damage thresholds, combo/spread checks, stale move table fields, and multi-player ratio/KOs logic.
Wrapped `fn_80038700` with `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop`, preserving it as a separate helper for summing integer ranges.

Changed `fn_8003CC84` digit extraction from `% 100` expressions to subtraction-based arithmetic: `(temp_ret - (temp_r5 * 100)) / 10` and `(temp_ret - (temp_r5 * 100)) - (temp_r3_3 * 10)`, which the bot reported completed that function match.

Refined `struct pl_804D6470_t` in `types.h` by replacing byte padding at known offsets with typed fields such as `float x0`, `float x8`, `u32 xC`, `float x10`, `float x20`, `u32 x50`, `u32 x54`, `u32 xF0`, and `float x160`.
The bot report said `.data` for `main/melee/pl/plbonus` improved from 0.00% to 100.00%, `.sdata2` from 57.89% to 100.00%, `fn_8003CC84` from 97.94% to 100.00%, and `fn_80039618` from 0.00% to 99.67%.
smart_moves: Used typed aliases for frequently accessed stat substructures and counters near the top of `fn_80039618`, reducing repeated deep field expressions while retaining exact offset-based behavior.
Kept the large bonus evaluator structured as direct condition checks against known table thresholds, which makes the decompiled logic searchable by flag ID and source statistic.
Introduced precise field types in `pl_804D6470_t` only where the new C needed comparisons or `pl_CalculateAverage` thresholds, avoiding a full speculative rename of the global config structure.
Used `#pragma dont_inline` on `fn_80038700`, a targeted compiler-control tactic to preserve expected function boundaries instead of contorting callers.
Rewrote `%` digit extraction into subtraction/division form, a small source-level expression change that matched the compiler output for `fn_8003CC84`.
Used explicit labels/gotos such as `skip_only_low_moves` and `skip_combo_count` where early-exit control flow likely better matched original branch structure.
decomp_lessons: For large stat/bonus functions, first expose enough struct field types to let comparisons compile naturally, then keep offset names until semantics are confirmed.
If a small helper starts disappearing into callers, `#pragma dont_inline` can be the least invasive way to preserve original code layout.
Mathematically equivalent digit or modulo expressions can produce different PowerPC codegen; try subtractive forms when `%` emits the wrong sequence.
Near-matching large functions may depend on control-flow shape as much as condition logic; explicit `goto` labels can be appropriate for matching early-exit loops.
Bot match reports can identify valuable partial work even on closed/unmerged drafts; here the reported 99.67% match for `fn_80039618` suggests the reconstruction was close but not finalized.
Be cautious interpreting this PR as accepted project state: it was a draft, closed unmerged, and has no human review comments in the available slice.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions kept address-based names such as `fn_80039618`, `fn_80038700`, and `fn_8003CC84`.

Known player API calls used existing PascalCase-style names such as `Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2`, `Player_GetActionStats`, `Player_GetEntity`, `Player_GetFalls`, and `Player_GetKOsByPlayerIndex`.
Unknown struct fields retained offset-style names such as `xC60`, `xD04`, `xD0C`, `xDD1`, `xDC8`, `xC74`, `xC78`, and `x160`.
Global config remained address/offset named as `pl_804D6470` and `pl_804D6470_t`, with newly typed fields still named by offset rather than guessed semantics.
Local labels were descriptive for branch purpose, e.g. `skip_only_low_moves` and `skip_combo_count`, without overclaiming game-specific bonus names.
Existing mixed naming was preserved: `setFlag` stayed lower camel case, while `pl_CalculateAverage` retained the module-prefixed exported-style name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied `#pragma push` / `#pragma dont_inline on` / `#pragma pop` around `fn_80038700` to influence inlining and function-boundary matching.
Replaced `% 100 / 10` and `% 100 % 10` with equivalent subtraction-based digit extraction to change generated arithmetic in `fn_8003CC84`.
Used `PAD_STACK(16)` at the end of `fn_80039618`, indicating stack layout padding was needed for closer matching.
Used direct casts between integer counts and `f32` in threshold comparisons, matching the diff's explicit `(f32)` conversions.
Used pointer iteration over `x358_counts` starting at `&x358_counts[1]` with a descending loop count, likely to match address-update and loop structure.

Used repeated calls to `Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2(player)` in some conditions instead of always reusing the earlier `table` local, suggesting source shape was tuned for register/codegen behavior.
Used range loops over action indices and explicit switch cases for sparse special indices, preserving observable branch/control-flow structure.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the available PR slice.
The only comment was an automated decomp.dev report showing +1640 matched code bytes, +456 matched data bytes, three new matches, and one unmatched-item improvement.
The PR was a draft and closed without merge, so its changes should be treated as useful matching evidence rather than accepted final code.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs touching `src/melee/pl/plbonus.c` and `fn_80039618` to see whether this closed draft was superseded by a merged version.
Look for subsequent renames or semantic documentation of `pl_804D6470_t` fields typed in this PR, especially offsets `x0`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x20`, `x50`, `x54`, `xF0`, and `x160`.
Compare later versions of `fn_8003CC84` to confirm whether the subtraction-based digit extraction was retained.
Investigate the remaining 0.33% mismatch in `fn_80039618` if no later PR completed it.
